Description:
**Project Summary Template: Ultra Vault Website & Digital Assets**

> What is Ultravault?

[International Private Vault and Safe Deposit Services - UltraVault](https://www.ultra-vault.com/)

---

**About Ultra Vault: Business Overview**

**What is Ultra Vault?**
Ultra Vault is a secure, global storage and logistics provider specializing in the safekeeping of precious metals, high-value assets, and confidential documents. The company offers clients private, insured vaulting solutions in multiple international locations, with a focus on security, discretion, and accessibility.

---

**Core Services:**

* **Physical Storage:** Ultra Vault provides state-of-the-art, high-security vaults for gold, silver, platinum, and other precious assets.
* **Global Access:** Clients can store and retrieve assets from various international vault locations, ensuring flexibility and convenience.
* **Logistics & Delivery:** The company manages secure transportation and delivery of assets worldwide.
* **Digital Integration:** Ultra Vault offers digital account management, allowing clients to monitor and manage their holdings online.
* **Compliance & Insurance:** All assets are fully insured, and the company adheres to strict compliance standards to protect client interests.

**Ultra Vault's Value Proposition:**

* **Security:** Industry-leading security protocols and facilities.
* **Privacy:** Discreet, confidential service for high-net-worth individuals and institutions.
* **Global Reach:** Access to a network of vaults in key financial centers.
* **Trust:** Transparent operations, full insurance, and regulatory compliance.

**Relevance to Our Project:**

* The new website and digital assets will showcase Ultra Vault's unique strengths and reassure clients about security and reliability.
* Technical integrations will streamline client onboarding and support, enhancing the overall customer experience.
* Clear, compelling content and design will help position Ultra Vault as a leader in secure asset storage and logistics, supporting business growth and client trust.

---

## Project breakdown: 

**Objectives**

* Develop a new website and digital assets for Ultra Vault to support business growth and compliance.
* Integrate technical features (API, billing, chatbot) and enhance user experience.
* Ensure alignment between RMD HK and Ultra Vault/MA HKG teams on deliverables and timelines.

---

**Scope**

* Website redesign and development
* Content creation - drafted by AI, finalised by client. 
* Technical integration (Techstack integration, chatbot setup, inquiry form structure)
* Internal QA, UAT, and post-launch support

| Tool | Purpose & Role | Key Benefits | How It Fits In | URL |

| **Webflow** | No-code website development platform | \- Rapid prototyping and deployment
- Full design control
- Built-in SEO tools & CMS
- Enables fast iteration by marketers/designers | Delivers beautiful, high-converting digital experiences quickly and flexibly | [https://webflow.com](<https://webflow.com>) |
| **Figma** | Collaborative UI/UX design | \- Real-time collaboration
- Centralized design systems
- Seamless handoff to dev
- Supports rapid iteration | Ensures brand and UI consistency; enables cross-team creative collaboration | [https://figma.com](<https://figma.com>) |
| **PostHog** | Product analytics & session replay | \- Deep user insight
- Session replays
- Feature flagging
- Usage-based pricing | Provides actionable data for product improvement and user experience optimization | [https://posthog.com](<https://posthog.com>) |
| **Clarify** | Autonomous CRM & revenue intelligence | \- Automated lead enrichment
- Tracks and syncs all conversations
- Unifies GTM data
- Automates follow-ups and admin | Keeps pipeline full, sales process efficient, and ensures no opportunity is missed | [https://clarify.ai](<https://clarify.ai>) |

---

**Key Updates (this cycle)**

* Project alignment document shared; awaiting management review (due within 5 business days)
* Figma design files and wireframes delivered for feedback
* Request sent for customer logos and English content
* Developer call to be scheduled for billing integration

---

**Tasks and Deadlines**

* Alignment doc review: within 5 business days
* Customer logos & English content: ASAP
* Developer call for billing: schedule this week
* Design feedback: by end of current cycle
* UAT & final review: before launch

---

**Access & Environments**

* Figma (designs)
* Google Drive (docs, assets)
* Staging environment for website testing (to be set up)

---

**Risks & Mitigations**

* **Delayed feedback:** Set clear deadlines, send reminders
* **Missing assets/content:** Escalate to stakeholders, provide checklists
* **Technical integration issues:** Early developer involvement, schedule regular syncs
* **Compliance gaps:** Involve compliance team in reviews

---

**Stakeholder Comms**

* Weekly project update emails
* Slack/Teams channel for quick queries
* Scheduled review calls after each major milestone

---

**Budget & Resources**

* Design & development hours allocated per phase
* Additional resources for content and technical integration as needed
* Budget tracking via project management tool

---

**Workstream Overview**

* **Discovery & Alignment:** Project kickoff, resource sharing, alignment doc review
* **Design & Content:** Sitemap, wireframes, content gathering, stakeholder feedback
* **Technical Integration:** Techstack integration, chatbot setup, inquiry form structure
* **Implementation & Testing:** Build, QA, UAT, stakeholder sign-off
* **Launch & Handover:** Go-live, documentation, training, post-launch support

Description:
# CarTracker Project Summary

## Project Overview

**Client:** CarTracker (via RMD)

**Scope:** Complete website redesign and repositioning

**Timeline:** 8-10 weeks (Dutch first, English rollout)

## Business Context

CarTracker provides fleet management and trip registration solutions in the Netherlands with two core products:

* **Trip Registration:** Single vehicle tracking (€12/month, primarily freelancers/small businesses)
* **Fleet Insight:** Multi-vehicle management (€7.25-€12/month based on contract terms)
* **Add-ons:** Dashcams and asset trackers as upsells

## Strategic Repositioning

**From:** Vehicle-focused service provider

**To:** Tech-first, continuously innovating fleet intelligence platform

**Key Differentiators:**

* In-house engineering team (7 developers/engineers)
* Rapid feature development and AI capabilities
* Transparent pricing (competitors hide pricing)
* Public development velocity (changelog, roadmap)

## Core Objectives

1. **Increase direct conversions** with streamlined checkout flows
2. **Position as innovation leader** through visible development cadence
3. **Expand market perception** beyond cars to all vehicles/equipment
4. **Optimize dual conversion paths** (purchase vs trial)

## Technical Requirements

* **Dual checkout flows:** Simplified purchase + soft-lead trial request
* **Custom JavaScript logic:** Cannot use native Webflow forms
* **Integrations:** GA4, Mollie (SEPA), ShotSpring (nurture)
* **Multi-asset support:** Cars, trucks, bikes, boats, special equipment

## Design Direction

* **Brand positioning:** Tech company aesthetic over vehicle imagery
* **Visual system:** Custom icons, duotone illustrations, route-line motifs
* **Color palette:** Black/white foundation with orange accents
* **Innovation proof:** Public changelog, optional roadmap with voting

## Conversion Strategy

* **Primary CTA:** Buy Now (direct purchase with 30-day trial period)
* **Secondary CTA:** Start 30-Day Test (soft lead for sales follow-up)
* **Path split:** 1 car (Trip Registration) vs Multiple cars (Fleet Insight)
* **Upsells:** Dashcams and asset trackers in checkout flow

## Success Metrics

* +25% increase in checkout starts within 8 weeks
* ≥10% soft-lead conversion rate
* ≥20% add-on attach rate for fleet orders
* ≥60% engagement with innovation content

## Current Status

**Phase:** Requirements gathering and stakeholder alignment

**Blockers:** GA4 access, sitemap confirmation, design inspiration references

**Next Milestone:** Design review in 2 weeks with revised homepage and flows

## Key Stakeholders

* **Geertjan:** Primary client contact and requirements owner
* **Christian:** Decision maker (currently traveling)
* **Daz:** Project lead and strategy 
* **Sean:** Design lead
* **Development team:** Custom implementation

## Risk Factors

* Design direction alignment without visual references
* Custom checkout complexity vs timeline
* Stakeholder availability for approvals
* Scope creep with additional features (changelog, roadmap)

Name: ORG-Gamma-CDPEngineering
Summary: Replace manual messaging with automated Customer.io + BigQuery CDP stack across Sleekflow/SimplyBook/web analytics. 2-week discovery starting now, then MVP build; fortnightly syncs
Description:
### ORG – Gamma: CDP Engineering — Executive Summary

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/0e79ed69-be03-43de-99fb-e32bd2cd116c/5444f6c2-1189-4ff0-ab3f-86438454fb00/a02357b3-8811-494f-9efc-9a79aa6c8c9a)

* **Objective**
  * Stand up a working CDP stack using [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) for orchestration and BigQuery as the warehouse, standardizing events/taxonomy across Sleekflow, SimplyBook, web (GA4/GTM), and ad platforms to replace semi‑manual messaging/exports with automated journeys and reliable pipelines. \[Discovery to focus on controllable components while Vault Dragon (MongoDB) timelines are confirmed.\]
* **Scope & Working Approach**
  * Two‑week discovery: confirm/access all systems, draft multi‑system data taxonomy (with PII flags/owners), ERD + identity strategy, and MVP pipeline/journey specs; proceed to BigQuery setup and CIO connection where feasible.
  * Focus first on Sleekflow/SimplyBook → BigQuery → [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>), plus web analytics alignment; coordinate with Vault Dragon for MongoDB access in their next sprint window.
* **Stakeholders & Cadence**
  * **Admiral**: Daz (PM), Davis (architect), Ghifari/Agif (data engineering), Rohan (commercial). ORG: Nicholas (decision maker), Bobby (project champion).
  * Fortnightly syncs (Tues 4PM) commencing Aug 26; limit wider stakeholder load during discovery to move quickly.
* **Commercials**
  * Base service: S$136,955 (CTC‑eligible) with FX buffer applied only on contract‑defined triggers; net‑30 monthly invoicing.
  * [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) billed via Airwallex virtual card.
  * Separate data consultant line: S$14k (S$7k upfront + S$7k completion) with \~S$6k buffer for dashboards; Admiral not liable for the consultant’s deliverables (document update requested).
* **Access & Current State**
  * Sleekflow: Admin/view access in place; single‑device login and 13‑seat cap; company inbox is primary channel; current automations semi‑manual; API key available.
  * SimplyBook: Access provided; enterprise upgrade to 500k API limit in progress; daily CSV→Google Sheets + Apps Script powers reminder workflow; complex service/provider/resource naming conventions.
  * [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>): Account previously suspended due to billing; reactivation in progress via ORG’s martech mailbox and card on file.
  * Analytics/ad platforms (GA4/GTM/GSC + ad accounts): to be granted for event taxonomy alignment and downstream activation.
* **Risks & Dependencies**
  * Vault Dragon sprint timing and access for MongoDB (PII handling, data model availability).
  * [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) reactivation/billing stability.
  * Sleekflow seat/device constraints and current semi‑manual processes; SimplyBook’s CSV/App Script dependence (need API/webhooks for reliability).
* **Near‑Term Priorities (next 10–14 days)**
  * Reactivate [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) and secure API keys; complete access to GA4/GTM/GSC and ad accounts.
  * Confirm Vault Dragon sprint window and request data model preview/integration options.
  * Publish multi‑system data taxonomy (with PII flags/owners), ERD + identity strategy.
  * Design ingestion pipelines for Sleekflow/SimplyBook → BigQuery (incremental loads + error handling), and define feed/mapping into [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) (profiles/events).
  * Document working agreements (RACI, SLAs), and track all work in Linear/Jira with intake → triage → delivery hygiene.
* **Expected Outcomes (Discovery → MVP)**
  * Clear, governed taxonomy and identity approach; reliable raw/stage/mart layers in BigQuery; automated ingestion for Sleekflow/SimplyBook; [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) connected with mapped attributes/events; MVP journeys specified (e.g., reminders, no‑show follow‑ups, reactivation) with measurable success metrics; risk/decision registers maintained and reviewed.

Description:
# RED CENTRE GLOBAL PROJECT BRIEF 

### Sprklabs | 11 November 2025 - 11 May 2026

---

## STRATEGIC ALIGNMENT

**Core Objective:** Build an AEO content engine that drives organic traffic while generating 5+ qualified leads per rolling quarter through topically-aligned lead magnets, supported by outreach ABM Campaigns

### Topical Pillars for SEO/AEO

1. **Advanced Negotiation Strategies** - Deal outcomes, value mapping
2. **Emotional Regulation for Leaders** - Composure under pressure
3. **Executive Conflict Resolution** - Cross-functional escalations
4. **High-Stakes Decision-Making** - Choices with incomplete data
5. **Leadership Frameworks** - Systematic approaches to team performance

---

## REFINED TARGET AUDIENCE

### Primary ICP: UK FinTech Revenue Leaders

**Why This ICP Works With Our Topics:**

* Face high-stakes negotiations daily (funding rounds, enterprise deals)
* Need emotional regulation (volatile markets, investor pressure)
* Navigate conflicts (sales vs product, client escalations)
* Make decisions with incomplete information (new markets, pricing)

### Secondary ICP: Fund Managers & PE Partners

**Natural Topic Alignment:**

* Complex multi-party negotiations
* High-pressure decision environments
* Stakeholder conflict management
* Leadership of portfolio companies

---

## DELIVERABLES (SIMPLIFIED)

### Phase 1: AEO Content Foundation (Weeks 1-4)

**3 Lead Magnets Aligned to Topical Strategy:**

1. **"The High-Stakes Negotiation Toolkit"**
   * Aligns with: Advanced negotiation strategies
   * Format: Interactive assessment + personalised playbook
   * AEO Target: "negotiation strategies for fintech leaders"
2. **"Pressure-Proof Decision Framework"**
   * Aligns with: Decision-making + Emotional regulation
   * Format: Decision tree template + stress-testing guide
   * AEO Target: "decision making under pressure finance"
3. **"The Conflict-to-Contract Accelerator"**
   * Aligns with: Conflict resolution + Leadership
   * Format: Escalation templates + resolution scorecards
   * AEO Target: "resolving stakeholder conflicts in deals"

### Phase 2: Content Scaling (Weeks 5-24)

**AEO Content Production:**

* 4 pillar pages per topical theme (20 total)

* Structured data markup for AI citations
* FAQ schemas for answer boxes

**Distribution Channels:**

* Organic SEO/AEO traffic to pillar content
* LinkedIn thought leadership posts
* Email nurture sequences per topic
* Retargeting based on content consumption

### Phase 3: Optimisation & Scale (Weeks 25-52)

**Performance Enhancement:**

* A/B test conversion paths per topic
* Refine based on citation performance
* Expand winning topics with clusters
* Build topical authority scores

---

## SUCCESS METRICS (CONTRACT-FOCUSED)

### Lead Generation Targets

* **Month 3:** 5 qualified leads (baseline = CHF 5,000 bonus)

* **Month 6:** 50+ ranking keywords, 5+ LLM citations

* Set up tracking infrastructure

* Keyword research per topic

**Sean:** Design assets, landing pages **Davis:** Technical setup, analytics tracking

Description:
## **AST-Account Care — General Project Brief**

**Summary:** Overall account support needs and logistics for former clients or ad‑hoc accounts not on retainer.

**Purpose:** Provide a single, organized lane for small support requests, troubleshooting, and minor updates for legacy or non‑paying accounts. This ensures continuity without disrupting priority delivery work.

**Scope:**

* Minor fixes, content tweaks, basic advisory, and light integrations.
* Requests should be under 4 hours; split larger asks and seek approval before proceeding.
* Excludes new feature builds, complex migrations, and multi‑week deliverables.

**Priority Policy:**

* This project operates at **Low priority** by default.
* Work is scheduled around active, revenue‑generating delivery commitments.
* Escalations require explicit approval from the project owner.

**SLA Guidance:**

* P2 Normal: target within 7 business days
* P3 Low: target within 30 days
* P0/P1 only with owner approval and confirmed commercial coverage

**Owner & Team:**

* Owner: **daz**
* Members: Delivery ops + relevant functional leads (add per request)

**Workflow:**

* Intake → Triage → In‑Progress → With Client → Verify/UAT → Done
* Use Blocked / Waiting on Client when applicable.

**Intake Template (issue):**

* Title: \[Account\] — \[Short request\]
* Context: URLs, assets, screenshots
* Impact/Risk: brief note
* Billing: Non‑paying / OOS pending / OOS approved
* SLA tier: P2/P3 by default

**Definition of Done:**

* Change applied and tested (staging/prod as applicable)
* Client notified with summary and links
* Billing notes updated (if any)
* Handover notes stored

Name: SB-StudioScents
Summary: Studio Scents: Mobile aromatherapy workshops serving Lappeenranta, Finland. Partners with wellness businesses offering scent therapy, candle making & perfume workshops. First & only provider in area.
Description:
# Studio Scents Business Brief

Based on our discussions with Monic about Studio Scents, here’s a comprehensive business analysis and strategic recommendations for her aromatherapy workshop business in **Lappeenranta, Finland.**

Watch through the brief below: 

[Screen Studio — Professional screen recorder for macOS](https://screen.studio/share/qTxBgI6e?state=uploading)

## **Current Business Overview**

**Status**: Early-stage startup with first client secured

**Location**: Lappeenranta, Finland

**Market Position**: First/only aromatherapy workshop provider in the local area

**Recent Milestone**: Successfully pitched and secured yoga/pilates studio partnership

## **Business Model Analysis**

### **Revenue Streams**

1. **B2B Partnerships** - Collaborations with wellness studios, spas, and fitness centers
2. **Workshop Services** - Three core offerings:
   * Home workshops (private/group sessions)
   * Scent candle workshops
   * Perfume workshops
3. **E-commerce** - Product sales through website with Webflow integration ( in phase 2, not now )

### **Service Delivery Model**

* **Mobile Workshop Service** - Monic brings aromatherapy experiences directly to partner locations
* **Custom Experience Design** - Tailored scent experiences for different business contexts
* **Educational Component** - Teaching participants about aromatherapy benefits and techniques

## **ICP & Buyer Personas**

### **Primary ICP: Wellness Business Owners**

**Demographics:**

* Location: Lappeenranta and surrounding regions
* Business type: Yoga studios, pilates studios, spas, wellness centers or any businesses that are SMBs/SMEs
* Business size: Small to medium (5-50 employees)
* Revenue: €50k-€500k annually

**Psychographics:**

* Values holistic wellness and unique customer experiences
* Seeks differentiation in competitive wellness market
* Open to innovative service partnerships
* Community-minded and locally focused

**Pain Points:**

* Need for unique offerings to differentiate from competitors
* Desire to enhance customer experience and retention
* Looking for additional revenue streams or partnership opportunities
* Want to provide more comprehensive wellness experiences

### **Secondary ICP: Corporate Wellness Programs**

**Target:** Local businesses seeking employee wellness initiatives \*\*

Decision Makers:\*\* HR managers, office managers, team leads

**Use Case:** Team building, stress relief workshops, corporate events

### **Tertiary ICP: Private Individuals**

**Target:** Health-conscious consumers seeking personal wellness experiences

**Context:** Birthday parties, bachelorette events, personal development

## **Competitive Positioning**

### **Unique Value Proposition**

*“The only comprehensive aromatherapy workshop experience in Lappeenranta - bringing personalized scent therapy directly to wellness businesses and their clients.”*

### **Key Differentiators**

1. **First-mover advantage** - No similar services currently available in Lappeenranta
2. **Mobile service model** - Brings experience to client locations
3. **B2B partnership focus** - Creates win-win scenarios for wellness businesses
4. **Educational approach** - Combines therapy with learning
5. **Local expertise** - Deep understanding of Finnish wellness culture

## **Recommended Positioning Strategy**

### **Market Position**: Pioneer Wellness Partner

Position Studio Scents as the innovative wellness solution that helps local businesses enhance their customer experience while introducing aromatherapy to the Lappeenranta market.

### **Brand Messaging Framework**

**Primary Message:**

*“Enhance your wellness business with Finland’s first mobile aromatherapy workshop experience.”*

**Supporting Messages:**

* **For Business Owners**: “Add a unique dimension to your wellness offerings without additional overhead”
* **For Customers**: “Discover the therapeutic power of personalized scent experiences”
* **For Community**: “Bringing ancient aromatherapy wisdom to modern Finnish wellness culture”

### **Value Propositions by Audience**

**For Wellness Studios:**

* Differentiate your studio with exclusive aromatherapy workshops
* Increase customer retention through unique experiences
* Generate additional revenue through workshop partnerships
* No equipment investment required - we bring everything

**For Participants:**

* Learn practical aromatherapy skills for daily wellness
* Create personalized scent products to take home
* Experience stress relief through guided scent therapy
* Small group, intimate learning environment

## **Go-to-Market Strategy for Lappeenranta**

### **Phase 1: Local Wellness Network (Months 1-3)**

* **Target**: 5-10 yoga/pilates studios, spas, wellness centers
* **Approach**: Direct outreach with pilot program offers
* **Goal**: Establish 3-5 regular partnership agreements

### **Phase 2: Corporate Wellness (Months 4-6)**

* **Target**: Local businesses with 10+ employees
* **Approach**: HR-focused workshops for employee wellness
* **Goal**: Monthly corporate workshops

### **Phase 3: Consumer Direct (Months 7-12)**

* **Target**: Private individuals for special occasions
* **Approach**: Referral program through business partners
* **Goal**: Weekly private workshops

### **Marketing Channels**

1. **Direct B2B Outreach** - Personal visits to wellness businesses
2. **Local Networking** - Entrepreneur events and business meetups
3. **Social Media** - Instagram/Facebook showcasing workshop experiences
4. **Referral Program** - Incentivize business partners to refer clients
5. **Community Events** - Participate in local wellness fairs and markets

## **Success Metrics & Milestones**

What the clients hope to achieve

**Short-term (3 months):**

* 3-5 regular business partnerships
* 20+ workshop sessions completed
* Website with booking system operational

**Medium-term (6 months):**

* €5,000+ monthly recurring revenue
* 10+ business partnerships
* Corporate client acquisition

**Long-term (12 months):**

* Market leader position in Lappeenranta
* €10,000+ monthly revenue
* Expansion consideration to neighboring cities

Description:
# Spireworks Support Brief

**Client Champion:** Pris (Priscilla Kwan)
**Lead:** Davis | **Support:** Elfajri
**Engagement:** Epsilon (Maintenance)

---

## Context

Spireworks migrated from v2.0 to v3.0. Five features from v2.0 are broken or missing in v3.0. Pris has recorded video walkthroughs for each issue.

**Tech Stack:** React + Firebase (Firestore) + Algolia
**Staging:** https://spireworks-357b2.web.app/
**Production:** https://spireworks-app.web.app/

---

## Issues Summary

as of Jan 21st 

| \# | Issue | Priority | Effort Est. | Video brief |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | IMEI Search - Partial search broken | P1 | Medium |  |
| 2 | Bulk Upload + File Attachment missing | P1 | Medium |  |
| 3 | Job Ref No not displaying | P2 | Low |  |
| 4 | S/N not clickable (IMEI works) | P2 | Low |  |
| 5 | Editable button - Admin only | P2 | Low |  |

---

## Issue Details

### 1\. IMEI Search Bar (Algolia)

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/bf186056-4301-4729-af43-050e27fd05aa>)

**Problem:** v3.0 requires full 15-digit IMEI to search. v2.0 allowed partial search (6+ digits).

**Expected:** Search with 6 digits → returns matching results
**Actual:** Search with partial digits → returns nothing

**Fix:** Check Algolia search config - likely needs prefix search or min character threshold adjustment.

---

### 2\. Bulk Upload + File Attachment

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/7b384425-7c15-490c-9731-3b63d7081643>)

**Problem A:** Bulk upload button missing in v3.0 (existed in v2.0 for uploading multiple IMEI numbers without manual entry).

**Problem B:** Cannot attach supporting documents during submission.

**Expected:**

* Bulk upload button to add multiple items
* File attachment field during submission

**Fix:** Restore bulk upload component + add file upload to submission form (Firebase Storage).

---

### 3\. Job Reference Number Display

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/cf55515d-8bf7-4ac6-8558-7c4d99f78abe>)

**Problem:** When viewing an inventory item, Job Ref No is not displayed in v3.0 (was visible in v2.0).

**Expected:** Job Ref No visible in item detail view
**Actual:** Field exists in data but not rendered

**Fix:** Add Job Ref No field to item detail component.

---

### 4\. Serial Number Clickable Link

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/3b4cf213-0879-4c40-8a45-a54ee759265a>)

**Problem:** In Job details, IMEI numbers are clickable (links to transaction history). Serial Numbers are not clickable.

**Expected:** Both IMEI and S/N should link to transaction history
**Actual:** Only IMEI is clickable

**Fix:** Apply same link logic to S/N field.

---

### 5\. Editable Button (Admin Restricted)

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/ef08b43a-3f00-4cc2-98c2-87fb27583e7b>)

**Problem:** Edit button in v2.0 allowed modifying details. Missing/broken in v3.0.

**Additional Request:** Restrict edit functionality to Pris's account only (security concern).

**Expected:** Edit button works, but only for admin (Pris)
**Actual:** Button missing or non-functional

**Fix:** Restore edit functionality + add role-based permission check (admin only).

---

# **1. Executive Summary**

## **1.1 Project Overview**

RMD HK Limited has been engaged by Hop Lun (HK) Ltd to deliver a comprehensive website redesign and development project. This Beta engagement will transform Hop Lun's digital presence through a modern, high-performance Webflow website that elevates their brand perception as a global leader in women's intimates and activewear manufacturing.

| **Client** | Hop Lun (HK) Ltd |
| -- | -- |
| **Agency** | RMD HK Limited |
| **Engagement Model** | Beta (Website Development) |
| **Timeline** | 16 weeks (21 Jan 2026 - 12 May 2026) |
| **Platform** | Webflow CMS |

## **1.2 Strategic Objectives**

1. Elevate Brand Perception: Position Hop Lun as a modern, innovative manufacturing leader differentiating from traditional competitors.
2. Implement Scaleable CMS: Deploy Webflow infrastructure enabling future multilingual capabilities and content management autonomy.
3. Drive Engagement: Create compelling user experiences supporting both commercial enquiries and HR recruitment initiatives.
4. Establish Analytics Foundation: Implement comprehensive tracking infrastructure for data-driven optimisation.

## **1.3 ICE Score Summary**

Overall Project ICE Score: 5.2 (Priority P1 - High)

* Impact: 8/10 - Significant brand elevation and commercial enablement for a 30-year manufacturing leader
* Confidence: 85% - Clear requirements, established technology stack, manageable scope
* Ease: 6/10 - Standard Beta complexity with Lottie animations and CMS configuration

# **2. Client Discovery & Strategic Context**

## **2.1 About Hop Lun**

Hop Lun (HK) Ltd is a global leader in women's intimates and activewear manufacturing with over 30 years of industry expertise. Operating 10+ fully owned and partner factories worldwide, they serve clients ranging from startups to established global brands, continuously raising industry benchmarks.

### **Core Value Proposition**

Speed | Quality | Competitive Pricing

### **Three Core Strengths**

* Design Expertise: In-house design studio with 150+ professionals across Hong Kong and China
* Manufacturing Excellence: Production capacity of 150M+ pieces annually with precision at scale
* Sustainability Leadership: Verified standards and responsible production practices

## **2.2 Competitive Landscape**

Primary competitors in the intimate apparel manufacturing sector:

| **Competitor** | **Position** |
| -- | -- |
| Cerie | Design-focused manufacturing |
| Crystal Martin | Quality-centric production |
| MAS Holdings | Scale and volume leader |
| Clover | Innovation-driven approach |
| Delta Bogart | Full-service solutions |

List of LIKE Websites:

* Brandix (brand visuals, interaction, consistency, usability) - [https://brandix.com/](<https://brandix.com/>)
  
  Why LIKE: Interactive animations: use tasteful micro-interactions to guide users without overwhelming them.
  Colour consistency: establish a clear, limited colour palette to reinforce brand recognition.
  User-friendly: intuitive navigation, clear CTAs, accessible design.
* Everlane (clean, neutral, transparency) - [https://www.everlane.com/pages/about](<https://www.everlane.com/pages/about>)
  
  Why LIKE: Factory storytelling: a dedicated, transparent narrative about production, ethics, and partners can build trust.
  Clean, neutral colour theme: light backgrounds with restrained accent colours for readability and focus.
* Inretrn (playful yet readable, bold colour, friendly shapes) - [https://inretrn.com/](<https://inretrn.com/>)
  
  Why LIKE: Colour scheme: yellow & black as a strong brand signal; high contrast for impact.
  Playful yet legible typography: rounded, friendly fonts that remain readable.
  Round shapes and corners: approachable UI elements (buttons, cards) to reduce friction.
  Dynamic hero/metrics: large moving figures or data visuals for a strong first impression.

List of HATE Websites:

* PRISM - [https://prism-on.tokyo/prism/](<https://prism-on.tokyo/prism/>)
  
  Why HATE: The design feels very playful with lots of movement and interactivity. While engaging, it’s making content hard to read and can be overwhelming for users due to rapid interactions. The colour palette feels too strong in places, which competes with the content. Moving elements are happening too quickly, which challenges navigation and reduces clarity.
* DePoly - [https://www.depoly.co/](<https://www.depoly.co/>)
  
  Why HATE: Multiple columns/pillars during scrolling create a reading pattern that feels disjointed. Users naturally read left-to-right; the current layout can be confusing if it disrupts that flow.
  The use of lines to separate information isn’t visually tidy and can create a cluttered impression.
* Cerie - [https://www.cerie.com/](<https://www.cerie.com/>)
  
  Why HATE: Notable as a competitor - Feels like a standard manufacturing site, lacks bold energy and distinctiveness.
* Mas - [https://masholdings.com/life-at-mas/](<https://masholdings.com/life-at-mas/>)
  
  Why HATE: Also a competitor - Similar page layouts across sections; can feel repetitive.
  Many sections display images/videos in a way that reinforces a “landing on the same page” feeling.

The website architecture is designed around a core conversion formula that maximises visitor-to-lead transformation:

***Conversion Rate = (Desire + Confidence) ÷ (Effort + Confusion)***

| **↑ Increase Desire** | **↑ Increase Confidence** | **↓ Decrease Effort** | **↓ Decrease Confusion** |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |

Name: ORG-Gamma-CDPEngineering
Summary: Replace manual messaging with automated Customer.io + BigQuery CDP stack across Sleekflow/SimplyBook/web analytics. 2-week discovery starting now, then MVP build; fortnightly syncs
Description:
### ORG – Gamma: CDP Engineering — Executive Summary

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/0e79ed69-be03-43de-99fb-e32bd2cd116c/5444f6c2-1189-4ff0-ab3f-86438454fb00/a02357b3-8811-494f-9efc-9a79aa6c8c9a)

* **Objective**
  * Stand up a working CDP stack using [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) for orchestration and BigQuery as the warehouse, standardizing events/taxonomy across Sleekflow, SimplyBook, web (GA4/GTM), and ad platforms to replace semi‑manual messaging/exports with automated journeys and reliable pipelines. \[Discovery to focus on controllable components while Vault Dragon (MongoDB) timelines are confirmed.\]
* **Scope & Working Approach**
  * Two‑week discovery: confirm/access all systems, draft multi‑system data taxonomy (with PII flags/owners), ERD + identity strategy, and MVP pipeline/journey specs; proceed to BigQuery setup and CIO connection where feasible.
  * Focus first on Sleekflow/SimplyBook → BigQuery → [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>), plus web analytics alignment; coordinate with Vault Dragon for MongoDB access in their next sprint window.
* **Stakeholders & Cadence**
  * **Admiral**: Daz (PM), Davis (architect), Ghifari/Agif (data engineering), Rohan (commercial). ORG: Nicholas (decision maker), Bobby (project champion).
  * Fortnightly syncs (Tues 4PM) commencing Aug 26; limit wider stakeholder load during discovery to move quickly.
* **Commercials**
  * Base service: S$136,955 (CTC‑eligible) with FX buffer applied only on contract‑defined triggers; net‑30 monthly invoicing.
  * [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) billed via Airwallex virtual card.
  * Separate data consultant line: S$14k (S$7k upfront + S$7k completion) with \~S$6k buffer for dashboards; Admiral not liable for the consultant’s deliverables (document update requested).
* **Access & Current State**
  * Sleekflow: Admin/view access in place; single‑device login and 13‑seat cap; company inbox is primary channel; current automations semi‑manual; API key available.
  * SimplyBook: Access provided; enterprise upgrade to 500k API limit in progress; daily CSV→Google Sheets + Apps Script powers reminder workflow; complex service/provider/resource naming conventions.
  * [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>): Account previously suspended due to billing; reactivation in progress via ORG’s martech mailbox and card on file.
  * Analytics/ad platforms (GA4/GTM/GSC + ad accounts): to be granted for event taxonomy alignment and downstream activation.
* **Risks & Dependencies**
  * Vault Dragon sprint timing and access for MongoDB (PII handling, data model availability).
  * [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) reactivation/billing stability.
  * Sleekflow seat/device constraints and current semi‑manual processes; SimplyBook’s CSV/App Script dependence (need API/webhooks for reliability).
* **Near‑Term Priorities (next 10–14 days)**
  * Reactivate [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) and secure API keys; complete access to GA4/GTM/GSC and ad accounts.
  * Confirm Vault Dragon sprint window and request data model preview/integration options.
  * Publish multi‑system data taxonomy (with PII flags/owners), ERD + identity strategy.
  * Design ingestion pipelines for Sleekflow/SimplyBook → BigQuery (incremental loads + error handling), and define feed/mapping into [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) (profiles/events).
  * Document working agreements (RACI, SLAs), and track all work in Linear/Jira with intake → triage → delivery hygiene.
* **Expected Outcomes (Discovery → MVP)**
  * Clear, governed taxonomy and identity approach; reliable raw/stage/mart layers in BigQuery; automated ingestion for Sleekflow/SimplyBook; [Customer.io](<http://Customer.io>) connected with mapped attributes/events; MVP journeys specified (e.g., reminders, no‑show follow‑ups, reactivation) with measurable success metrics; risk/decision registers maintained and reviewed.

# PROJECT BRIEF: Engineering Mastery Programme

## From Business Leader to Technical Commander

**Project Codename:** Operation Technical Transformation
**Duration:** 180 Days (6 Months)
**Daily Commitment:** 30 Minutes
**Total Investment:** 90 Hours
**Expected ROI:** 10x Enterprise Value Multiple Enhancement

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

### Mission Statement

Transform from a business-first leader into a technically-proficient commander capable of building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered revenue automation tools that drive 60-80% gross margins whilst enabling the shift to 50% recurring product revenue.

### Strategic Objectives

1. **Technical Proficiency:** Achieve intermediate-level engineering skills in Python, AI/ML integration, and full-stack development
2. **Revenue Generation:** Build 5+ production-ready tools that directly impact enterprise value
3. **Operational Excellence:** Automate 40% of current manual processes across α-ε engagement tiers
4. **Platform Creation:** Develop 3+ white-label SaaS products for recurring revenue streams
5. **Team Enablement:** Create technical foundation for SG-ID distributed teams

### Success Metrics

* **Technical KPIs:** 50+ commits, 10+ deployed applications, 5+ API integrations
* **Business KPIs:** 30% margin improvement, 25% operational cost reduction, 3 new ε-tier products
* **Grant Maximisation:** SGD 100K+ in IMDA/EDG grant eligibility through documented innovation

---

## STAKEHOLDER ANALYSIS

### Primary Stakeholder (You - The Commander)

* **Current State:** Business strategist with limited technical implementation capability
* **Desired State:** Full-stack engineer capable of rapid prototyping and deployment
* **Gap Analysis:** 90 hours of structured learning + 100 hours of practical application

### Secondary Stakeholders

* **SG-ID Teams:** Need automated workflows and clear technical documentation
* **Enterprise Clients (δ-tier):** Expect AI-powered solutions and innovation
* **Platform Users (ε-tier):** Require scalable, self-serve products

Description:
# RED CENTRE GLOBAL PROJECT BRIEF 

### Sprklabs | 11 November 2025 - 11 May 2026

---

## STRATEGIC ALIGNMENT

**Core Objective:** Build an AEO content engine that drives organic traffic while generating 5+ qualified leads per rolling quarter through topically-aligned lead magnets, supported by outreach ABM Campaigns

### Topical Pillars for SEO/AEO

1. **Advanced Negotiation Strategies** - Deal outcomes, value mapping
2. **Emotional Regulation for Leaders** - Composure under pressure
3. **Executive Conflict Resolution** - Cross-functional escalations
4. **High-Stakes Decision-Making** - Choices with incomplete data
5. **Leadership Frameworks** - Systematic approaches to team performance

---

## REFINED TARGET AUDIENCE

### Primary ICP: UK FinTech Revenue Leaders

**Why This ICP Works With Our Topics:**

* Face high-stakes negotiations daily (funding rounds, enterprise deals)
* Need emotional regulation (volatile markets, investor pressure)
* Navigate conflicts (sales vs product, client escalations)
* Make decisions with incomplete information (new markets, pricing)

### Secondary ICP: Fund Managers & PE Partners

**Natural Topic Alignment:**

* Complex multi-party negotiations
* High-pressure decision environments
* Stakeholder conflict management
* Leadership of portfolio companies

---

## DELIVERABLES (SIMPLIFIED)

### Phase 1: AEO Content Foundation (Weeks 1-4)

**3 Lead Magnets Aligned to Topical Strategy:**

1. **"The High-Stakes Negotiation Toolkit"**
   * Aligns with: Advanced negotiation strategies
   * Format: Interactive assessment + personalised playbook
   * AEO Target: "negotiation strategies for fintech leaders"
2. **"Pressure-Proof Decision Framework"**
   * Aligns with: Decision-making + Emotional regulation
   * Format: Decision tree template + stress-testing guide
   * AEO Target: "decision making under pressure finance"
3. **"The Conflict-to-Contract Accelerator"**
   * Aligns with: Conflict resolution + Leadership
   * Format: Escalation templates + resolution scorecards
   * AEO Target: "resolving stakeholder conflicts in deals"

### Phase 2: Content Scaling (Weeks 5-24)

* 4 pillar pages per topical theme (20 total)

**Distribution Channels:**

* Organic SEO/AEO traffic to pillar content

* Retargeting based on content consumption

### Phase 3: Optimisation & Scale (Weeks 25-52)

* A/B test conversion paths per topic
* Refine based on citation performance

* Build topical authority scores

Name: SPI-Epsilon-WebApp
Summary: Spireworks is a returning client since 2023 for whom we built an inventory management system used by their logistics team. They recently migrated from v2.0 to v3.0, which broke five features that Pris (their project champion) has flagged as blocking daily
Description:
# Spireworks Support Brief

**Client Champion:** Pris (Priscilla Kwan)
**Lead:** Davis | **Support:** Elfajri
**Engagement:** Epsilon (Maintenance)

---

## Context

Spireworks migrated from v2.0 to v3.0. Five features from v2.0 are broken or missing in v3.0. Pris has recorded video walkthroughs for each issue.

**Tech Stack:** React + Firebase (Firestore) + Algolia
**Staging:** https://spireworks-357b2.web.app/
**Production:** https://spireworks-app.web.app/

---

## Issues Summary

as of Jan 21st 

| \# | Issue | Priority | Effort Est. | Video brief |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | IMEI Search - Partial search broken | P1 | Medium |  |
| 2 | Bulk Upload + File Attachment missing | P1 | Medium |  |
| 3 | Job Ref No not displaying | P2 | Low |  |
| 4 | S/N not clickable (IMEI works) | P2 | Low |  |
| 5 | Editable button - Admin only | P2 | Low |  |

---

## Issue Details

### 1\. IMEI Search Bar (Algolia)

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/bf186056-4301-4729-af43-050e27fd05aa>)

**Problem:** v3.0 requires full 15-digit IMEI to search. v2.0 allowed partial search (6+ digits).

**Expected:** Search with 6 digits → returns matching results
**Actual:** Search with partial digits → returns nothing

**Fix:** Check Algolia search config - likely needs prefix search or min character threshold adjustment.

---

### 2\. Bulk Upload + File Attachment

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/7b384425-7c15-490c-9731-3b63d7081643>)

**Problem A:** Bulk upload button missing in v3.0 (existed in v2.0 for uploading multiple IMEI numbers without manual entry).

**Problem B:** Cannot attach supporting documents during submission.

**Expected:**

* Bulk upload button to add multiple items
* File attachment field during submission

**Fix:** Restore bulk upload component + add file upload to submission form (Firebase Storage).

---

### 3\. Job Reference Number Display

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/cf55515d-8bf7-4ac6-8558-7c4d99f78abe>)

**Problem:** When viewing an inventory item, Job Ref No is not displayed in v3.0 (was visible in v2.0).

**Expected:** Job Ref No visible in item detail view
**Actual:** Field exists in data but not rendered

**Fix:** Add Job Ref No field to item detail component.

---

### 4\. Serial Number Clickable Link

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/3b4cf213-0879-4c40-8a45-a54ee759265a>)

**Problem:** In Job details, IMEI numbers are clickable (links to transaction history). Serial Numbers are not clickable.

**Expected:** Both IMEI and S/N should link to transaction history
**Actual:** Only IMEI is clickable

**Fix:** Apply same link logic to S/N field.

---

### 5\. Editable Button (Admin Restricted)

**Video:** [Link](<https://priscilla-kwan.neetorecord.com/admin/recordings/ef08b43a-3f00-4cc2-98c2-87fb27583e7b>)

**Problem:** Edit button in v2.0 allowed modifying details. Missing/broken in v3.0.

**Additional Request:** Restrict edit functionality to Pris's account only (security concern).

**Expected:** Edit button works, but only for admin (Pris)
**Actual:** Button missing or non-functional

**Fix:** Restore edit functionality + add role-based permission check (admin only).

---

## RACI

| Role | Person |
| -- | -- |
| **Responsible** | Elfajri |
| **Accountable** | Davis |
| **Consulted** | Daz |
| **Informed** | Pris |

* **Firebase Console:** devdev.spire@gmail.com
* **Algolia Dashboard:** devdev.spire@gmail.com

## Next Steps

1. Davis to review videos and confirm estimates
2. Elfajri to set up local dev environment
3. Tackle P1 issues first (Search + Bulk Upload)
4. Demo fixes to Pris on staging before production deploy

Meeting about data tracking standards (20 Feb 2026)

Participants: Davis & Shafiq

**Analytics Tracking Implementation Strategy**

* Consideration of when to use which analytics tracking installation:
  * Use respective tool script tagging for standard implementations
    * Content Square, GA4, PostHog, Fullstory, etc.
    * Auto-tagging approach for basic page views and scroll depth
  * Custom tracking code from Admiral Systems for advanced needs
    * Depends on SOW requirements
    * Manual event tagging for mission-critical events (B2B form submissions, ecommerce purchases)
    * Commercials must charge extra for this implementation

### **CDP and Identity Resolution Challenges**

* Segment limitations for current business model
  * Too expensive for SME clients Admiral Systems now targets
  * Last referral from Segment was 2-3 years ago
  * Not commercially viable as channel partner
  * Identity resolution capabilities still best-in-class
* PostHog CDP alternative
  * Data pipelines only, no identity resolution
  * Risk for clients needing profile merging across devices
  * Suitable only when clients explicitly need data pipelines without identity resolution

### **Custom Tracking Code Redesign (V3)**

Note: We are planning to develop custom code tracking (which can integrate to CSQ, PostHog, GA4, etc) in the future.

* Current V2 approach is segment-first, needs abstraction
* Proposed solution using Analytics.js as foundation
  * NPM package, last published 6 months ago
  * Use as baseline with custom wrapper on top
  * AI-assisted development to recreate functionality
* Implementation maintains data attribute approach
  * Events defined via custom HTML attributes rather than code
  * Page view events: data-event on body tag
  * Click events: data-event on buttons/links
  * Form submissions: data-identify and data-track attributes
  * CMS integration: data-property for dynamic content

### **Team Development and Process Standardization**

* Current junior team struggling with basic Webflow fundamentals
  * Missing framework for problem-solving
  * Need business context understanding beyond just coding
  * Exposure to senior workflow methods showing significant improvement
* Best practice for event tracking implementation:
  * Data taxonomy and event tracking plan should be source of truth
  * Strategy team defines what to track with client
  * Senior technical team creates event tracking plan
  * Junior team executes implementation
  * Sprint-style planning approach
* Weekly training sessions planned
  * Safe space for questions without judgment
  * Compile questions for efficient group sessions
  * Starting next Thursday 5pm Singapore time

### **Next Steps**

* Shafiq to send calendar invite for weekly junior team training session
* Start creating standardized documentation for common Webflow implementations

# 4Walls Growth Partnership — Project Brief

**Client:** 4 Walls Agents Ltd — Oliver Thomas Lawson Bates
**Supplier:** Valentin Pietra — Sprklabs
**Document Date:** 11 Feb 2026 · Version 1.0
**Engagement Start:** 23 Feb 2026

> Note: This is partnership pricing for a referred client relationship.

---

+++ ## 1\. Problem Statement

4Walls has built a sophisticated custom tech stack (proprietary CRM, CMS, and booking system) that handles lead capture and property listings. The missing piece is an **automation layer** that converts those leads into booked viewings at scale without manual intervention.

| Problem | Pain Level | Current Impact |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 50% of bookings require manual calls | **Critical** | You're the bottleneck; can't scale |
| No cross-sell automation | **Critical** | Leaving viewings (and sales) on the table |
| Email open rates are low | **High** | Missing warm leads who go cold |
| No segmented buyer communication | **High** | One-size-fits-all messaging underperforms |
| Cashflow timing concerns | **High** | Hard to invest ahead of revenue |
| No property search/filters on site | **Medium** | Buyers bounce; competitors capture them |
| CRM attribution unclear | **Medium** | Can't tell what's working |

---

+++

+++ ## 2\. Engagement Focus

1. **Lifecycle Marketing Optimization** — Improving email (Loops) and WhatsApp (ManyChat) sequences to increase conversion without manual calls
2. **Cross-Sell Automation** — Building "you viewed X, consider Y" flows to maximise viewings per lead
3. **Analytics & Attribution** — Setting up proper tracking to measure what's working
4. **Design Advisory** — Providing UX recommendations that 4Walls' team implements

---

+++

+++ ## 3\. Scope of Services

### Conversion Layer: Email & Automation Overhaul

| Problem | Solution | Deliverable |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Email open rates are low | Deliverability audit + optimisation | Technical fixes + subject line testing framework |
| One-size-fits-all messaging | Intent-based segmentation | 3 buyer segments with tailored sequences |
| No nurture for non-ready buyers | Long-term nurture sequences | 6-month drip campaign for "not yet" buyers |
| Leads go cold between touchpoints | Multi-channel coordination | WhatsApp + Email orchestration strategy |

> Sprklabs will coordinate with 4Walls on any CMS/CRM technical implementation.

### Automation Layer: Bookings on Autopilot

| Problem | Solution | Deliverable |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 50% of bookings require manual calls | Automated booking sequences | End-to-end booking flow via ManyChat + Loops |
| No cross-sell for multiple viewings | "You viewed X, consider Y" automation | Cross-development recommendation engine |
| No accountability for lead response | Speed-to-lead automation | Instant response + follow-up sequences |
| Don't know which automations drove sales | Full attribution tracking | CRM tagging system + monthly attribution report |

> Sprklabs will coordinate with 4Walls on any CMS/CRM technical implementation.

---

+++

+++ ## 4\. Responsibilities Split

| Sprklabs | 4Walls |
| -- | -- |
| Provide Figma mockups for UX improvements | Implement code changes |
| Specify event tracking requirements | Add ID tags and GTM snippets to pages |
| Design & implement email + WhatsApp sequences | — |
| Recommend CRM tagging taxonomy | Implement tags in custom CRM |
| Request form field changes | Add fields + integrate with CRM |

---

+++

+++ ## 5\. Implementation Timeline

### Month 1: Discovery & Foundation

**23 Feb – 22 Mar 2026**

#### Week 1–2: Access & Audit (23 Feb – 8 Mar)

* Get access to Loops, ManyChat, GA4, GSC, ContentSquare
* Get view access to CRM + Figma designs
* Audit existing email sequences and WhatsApp flows
* Audit existing design and planned improvements (landing pages, forms, booking flow, Figma designs for property search, questionnaires, case studies)
* Document baseline metrics (conversion rates, open rates, manual booking ratio)

#### Week 3–4: Strategy & Quick Wins (9 Mar – 22 Mar)

* Define buyer segments (by intent, timeline, budget)
* Design attribution taxonomy for CRM (4Walls implements)
* Implement quick wins (deliverability fixes, subject line tests)
* Set up ContentSquare tracking

---

### Months 2–4: Automation Build

**23 Mar – 22 Jun 2026 (Weeks 5–16)**

* Build segmented email sequences in Loops (welcome, nurture, re-engagement)
* Optimise ManyChat booking flows
* Implement speed-to-lead automation
* Launch A/B testing framework
* Provide Figma mockups for priority UX improvements (4Walls implements)

### Months 5–6: Optimisation & Expansion

**23 Jun – 22 Aug 2026**

* Refine based on performance data
* Explore AEO/SEO opportunities (secondary channel)
* Monthly reporting and KPI reviews

---

### Months 7–13: Scale & Maintain

**23 Aug 2026 – 22 Feb 2027**

* Update automations for new developments
* Scale what's working
* Prepare for increased lead volume

| Design Reviews | As needed | Virtual meeting or email | Visual approval and feedback |

### Decision Framework

| Decision Type | Owner | Response Timeline | Process |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Initial Design Review / Approval | 4Walls | 3 business days | Presentation → Feedback → Revision |
| Design Iteration (1x) Review / Approval | 4Walls | 2 business days | Presentation → Feedback → Touch-ups → Approval |
| Content Review | 4Walls | 5 business days | Draft → Review → Edits → Final |
| Technical Decisions | Sprklabs | Same day | Recommendation → Discussion → Implementation |

### Change Management Process

1. **Change Request Submission** — Requesting party submits a formal Change Request Form by email
2. **Impact Analysis** — Sprklabs assesses impact on scope, schedule, resources, and cost
3. **Review and Approval** — Both parties must review and approve the change request
4. **SOW Amendment** — Approved changes documented in a formal SOW amendment
5. **Implementation** — Change implemented once approved and documented

---

+++

+++ ## 9\. Acceptance & Testing

### Acceptance Process

1. Sprklabs presents the deliverable to the client
2. Client provides review and feedback within the agreed timeline
3. If no feedback is provided within the agreed timeline, deliverable is **automatically accepted**
4. If feedback is provided, Sprklabs addresses it within the scope of the SOW
5. Once feedback is addressed, client has **1 business day** for final acceptance

### Testing Methodology

* **Development Testing** — Sprklabs performs internal testing before submission
* **User Acceptance Testing (UAT)** — Client tests implementation against defined requirements
* **Performance Testing** — Speed, load times, and mobile responsiveness tested against industry benchmarks
* **Integration Testing** — All system integrations (email marketing, analytics, etc.) tested for proper data flow

---

+++

+++ ## 10\. Assumptions & Exclusions

### Key Assumptions

 1. **Dedicated Point of Contact** — Client assigns a named decision-maker to collaborate with Sprklabs throughout
 2. **Timely Feedback** — Delays in review or milestone acceptance may extend delivery timelines proportionally
 3. **Meeting Availability** — Client team available for regular strategy and ad-hoc meetings
 4. **Platform Access** — 4Walls provides access to Loops, ManyChat, GA4, GSC, ContentSquare, CRM (view), and Figma within **5 business days of contract signature**. If not provided, Sprklabs may invoice the setup fee but delay work start; timelines shift accordingly
 5. **Media & Brand Assets** — Client provides all required assets in a timely manner; stock/paid imagery not included
 6. **Copy** — Sprklabs provides one round of copy (draft); client reviews and amends
 7. **Third-Party Costs** — All third-party tool fees paid directly by the client
 8. **Digital Advertising** — Ad spend managed and paid by the client; not in scope
 9. **Mutual Transparency (Performance Model)**
    * Sprklabs provides: real-time dashboard/data access, weekly lead status reports, full campaign metrics
    * 4Walls provides: CRM access showing lead progression, feedback on lead quality, monthly sales activity summary
    * Shared responsibility for maintaining updated lead status and attribution tracking
10. **Code Warranty** — 90-day warranty from go-live on Sprklabs-authored code; excludes third-party code or client modifications
11. **Technical Implementation** — All code changes, landing page updates, and CRM modifications implemented by 4Walls' team. Sprklabs specifies; 4Walls builds. Delays in 4Walls implementation proportionally extend Sprklabs deliverable timelines
12. **Custom CRM Integration** — Attribution tracking designed by Sprklabs, implemented by 4Walls; tagging taxonomy agreed before implementation
13. **AEO/SEO is Secondary** — Included but not the primary focus; primary KPI is reducing manual bookings and increasing automated viewings

### Out of Scope

* Any development work (4Walls team handles all code)
* CRM development or modification
* Photography or video production
* Paid advertising management
* Social media management
* Translation services
* Legal or financial consulting
* Sales calls and closing activities (remain with the client)
* Custom integrations beyond standard tools
